label: CATL Headquarters & Gigafactory
sub: Ningde, Fujian, China — World's largest single battery maker; >600 GWh/yr capacity. Produces LFP, NMC, NMCA, and sodium-ion cells. Home of Kirin and Shenxing battery R&D.

label: Tesla Gigafactory Nevada
sub: Sparks, Nevada, USA — Panasonic-Tesla JV. Produces 2170 cylindrical cells for Model 3/Y and Powerwall/Megapack. Opened 2016; expanded multiple times. ~40 GWh/yr capacity.

label: Tesla Gigafactory Texas
sub: Austin, Texas, USA — Opened April 2022. First production site for Tesla's 4680 tabless cylindrical cells and structural battery packs. Produces Cybertruck and Model Y. ~100 GWh/yr target.

label: Tesla Gigafactory Berlin-Brandenburg
sub: Grünheide, Germany — Inaugurated March 2022. Produces Model Y for European market with structural battery packs. First Tesla factory outside the Americas. ~500,000 vehicles/yr target.

label: Northvolt Revolt Ett — Skellefteå
sub: Skellefteå, Sweden — First European-owned gigafactory (first cell Dec 2021). Powered by 100% renewable hydropower. Filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy Nov 2024 amid quality and yield failures; restructuring ongoing.

label: LG Energy Solution — Ochang Campus
sub: Ochang, North Chungcheong, South Korea — Major LGES production hub for cylindrical and pouch cells. Supplies Tesla, GM (Ultium), Stellantis. After landmark Jan 2022 IPO, LGES is now second-largest battery maker globally.

label: Samsung SDI — Cheonan Factory
sub: Cheonan, South Korea — Major Samsung SDI prismatic and cylindrical cell production. Partners with BMW, Stellantis, Rivian. Leading solid-state battery R&D program targeting 2027–2030 pilot production.

label: BYD Headquarters & Blade Battery Production
sub: Shenzhen, Guangdong, China — Home of BYD's corporate HQ and key R&D campus. Blade Battery (LFP) invented and commercialized here (2020). BYD is now the world's largest EV maker by volume and a top-3 battery maker.

label: Volkswagen PowerCo — Salzgitter
sub: Salzgitter, Lower Saxony, Germany — VW's battery subsidiary PowerCo SE established 2022. Unified prismatic cell production targeting 40 GWh/yr. Anchor of VW's six-gigafactory European plan.

label: Toyota Solid-State Battery R&D — Toyota City
sub: Toyota City, Aichi, Japan — Toyota holds ~1,000 solid-state battery patents — more than any other automaker. Sulphide solid electrolyte pilot production began early 2025. Targeting 2027–2028 commercial EV with 1,200 km range.

label: QuantumScape — San Jose, CA
sub: San Jose, California, USA — Stanford spin-off backed by VW and Bill Gates. Lithium-metal solid-state separator pioneer. IPO via SPAC in 2020, briefly valued at $50B. Achieved automotive-format cell spec in 2024 (QSE-5).

label: Argonne National Lab — Battery Research
sub: Lemont, Illinois, USA — US DOE national lab and home of the Joint Center for Energy Storage Research (JCESR). Leads US government-funded battery research including lithium-sulfur, lithium-air, and solid-state chemistries. Budget >$120M/yr in battery R&D.

label: Samsung Advanced Institute of Technology (SAIT)
sub: Suwon, Gyeonggi, South Korea — Samsung's flagship research center. Key solid-state battery research including sulphide electrolyte and lithium-metal anode. Published peer-reviewed solid-state cycle-life breakthroughs. Works closely with Samsung SDI on commercialization.

label: Atacama Salt Flat — Lithium Brine
sub: Atacama, Antofagasta, Chile — World's largest lithium reserves (~37% global supply) in brine deposits. SQM and Albemarle produce lithium carbonate here. Chile nationalized lithium industry in 2023 (CODELCO joint ventures). ~200,000 tonne LCE/yr capacity.

label: Pilbara — Hard Rock Lithium Spodumene
sub: Pilbara, Western Australia — World's largest hard-rock lithium source. Pilbara Minerals, Albemarle (Greenbushes), and IGO operate mines. Australia is the world's largest lithium producer by volume (~50% of global mine supply). Spodumene is processed into hydroxide for NMC batteries.

label: DRC Cobalt Belt — Katanga
sub: Katanga Province, Democratic Republic of Congo — Source of ~70% of global cobalt. Glencore (Katanga Mining) and Chinese companies (CMOC) are dominant operators. Human rights abuses in artisanal mining (ASM) operations have prompted automakers to require supply chain due diligence and accelerate cobalt-free battery development.

label: Moss Landing BESS — 3 GWh
sub: Moss Landing, Monterey County, California, USA — Vistra Energy's facility is the world's largest grid-scale battery (3 GWh after Phase 3 expansion, 2025). Uses Tesla Megapacks. Replaced two gas peaker plants. Demonstrates 4-hour discharge for 300,000+ homes.

label: Hornsdale Power Reserve — Neoen/Tesla
sub: Jamestown, South Australia — Built in 2017, Neoen's Hornsdale Power Reserve was the world's first large-scale lithium-ion grid battery (150 MW / 194 MWh). Proved grid storage commercial viability. Expanded in 2020 to 300 MW / 394 MWh. Demonstrated frequency response 140× faster than gas turbines.

label: US Department of Energy — Washington, DC
sub: Washington, DC, USA — DOE administers the IRA's $3B battery manufacturing grants, the 45X production tax credit, and the Loan Programs Office (LPO) financing for battery factories. JCESR and Battery500 Consortium funded by DOE. Under Biden administration, issued $10B+ in battery facility loans.

label: European Commission — Brussels
sub: Brussels, Belgium — EU Battery Regulation (2023) mandates digital battery passport, carbon footprint disclosure, and recycled content minimums. European Battery Alliance (EBA250) coordinates EU gigafactory strategy. EU Critical Raw Materials Act (2024) targets 10% domestic extraction of battery minerals by 2030.
